title: SIGILS IN STATIC
subtitle: Static Drive-In · 10:48 PM · between features
epigraph_upright: The dreaming as honest discipline. The symbols that arrive at night and ask you to write them down. The Moon upright is the pattern read for what it is — a pattern — and noted, and walked away from.
epigraph_reversed: The pattern that won't let go. The static that reads like a sentence because you have been awake too long and the night is doing its work without you. The Moon reversed is the meaning-making that has stopped being a stance and started being the room.
scene_description: 10:48 PM at the Static Drive-In, the single-screen lot at the north end of Lacombe. Tonight's first feature ended at 10:38; the second starts at 11:00. The screen is blank — white, lot-side-up. The eight-row lot is half-full of pickups and sedans. The concession is yours tonight. You are also, in your off-hours, eight months into a documentary about late-night radio sigils, and the documentary has, since June, been doing a thing to your sleep. Your handheld is in your apron pocket. Between features you have twelve minutes.
direction_hint: Run the concession through the break. Pop the next batch. Pour twelve sodas. Restock the candy case. Ring the bell at 10:58 to call people back to their cars. Don't watch the static on the screen for more than the count of three. Don't, when the static makes the shape you have been seeing, write it down in your notebook.
opening_log_lines: 10:48 PM. The screen is blank. The lot is half-full.
Twelve minutes. Then the second feature.
Click [b]PHASE: ACTION[/b] anytime for the rules. [b]MOVE ↪[/b] to cross the snack bar.

inertia_thresholds.label: the static has a shape twice
the static spells a letter you know
the pattern matches the one from June
the pen is in your hand

thresholds.label: LOT PORCH
thresholds.flavor: Out the front of the snack bar. The lot. The pickups.

notes: Moon scenario. Win = run the concession (POP CORN twice, POUR SODA twice, restock candy), RING BELL at 10:58, connect 2 visitors, keep MOONDRIFT below 8 at close. Loss: drift pin at 12, OR you wrote the pattern in your notebook (the documentary takes you).
